Finals Schedule

March 16–20

Class start timeClasses which include Tuesday or Thursday meetings (MTWRF, MTWR, MTRF, MTWF, MWRF, TR, TRF, RF)Classes which include neither Tuesday nor Thursday meetings (MWF, MW, WF, MF, etc...)
Between
8:00 and 8:29 AM		Friday
8:00 - 10:00 AM		Friday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between
8:30 and 9:29 AM		Thursday
8:00 - 10:00 AM		Thursday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between
9:30 and 10:29 AM		Monday
8:00 - 10:00 AM		Monday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between
10:30 and 11:29 AM		Tuesday
8:00 - 10:00 AM		Tuesday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between
11:30 and 12:29 PM		Monday
1:00 - 3:00 PM		Monday
3:30 - 5:30 PM
Between
12:30 and 1:29 PM		Wednesday
8:00 - 10:00 AM		Wednesday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between
1:30 and 2:29 PM		Tuesday
1:00 - 3:00 PM		Tuesday
3:30 - 5:30 PM
Between
2:30 and 3:29 PM		Wednesday
1:00 - 3:00 PM		Wednesday
3:30 - 5:30 PM
Between
3:30 and 4:29 PM		Thursday
1:00 - 3:00 PM		Thursday
3:30 - 5:30 PM
Between
4:30 and 5:59 PM		Tuesday
6:00 - 8:00 PM		Monday
6:00 - 8:00 PM

Special Conditions:

  • Classes beginning at 6:00 p.m. or later will have their final exams during finals week at the regularly scheduled time. For example, a class that meets TR 6:00–9:00 PM will have its final exam on Tuesday of finals week from 6:00–8:00 PM. The exam period should not exceed two hours.
  • For classes involving both lecture and lab, the final exam will be based only upon the meeting times and days for the lecture portion of the class.
  • The final exam for a class that meets only as a lab should be at the scheduled time during finals week.
  • Two-hour classes and classes starting on the hour but extending beyond the next hour should schedule exams as if the class met only during the first hour.
  • Remote-Asynchronous Classes: Please refer to your syllabus or the instructor for details about final exams in asynchronous classes.
  • If the final examinations schedule causes students to take three or more examinations in one day, any of their instructors may arrange an examination later during finals week

June 8–12

1:00 - 3:00 PM		Tuesday
3:30 - 5:30 PM
Between

8:00 - 10:00 AM		Wednesday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between

8:00 - 10:00 AM		Friday
10:30 - 12:30 PM
Between
4:30 and 5:59 PM		Tuesday
6:00 - 8:00 PM		Monday
6:00 - 8:00 PM

Special Conditions:

  • Classes beginning at 6:00 p.m. or later will have their final exams during finals week at the regularly scheduled time. For example, a class that meets TR 6:00–9:00 PM will have its final exam on Tuesday of finals week from 6:00–8:00 PM. The exam period should not exceed two hours.
  • For classes involving both lecture and lab, the final exam will be based only upon the meeting times and days for the lecture portion of the class.
  • The final exam for a class that meets only as a lab should be at the scheduled time during finals week.
  • Two-hour classes and classes starting on the hour but extending beyond the next hour should schedule exams as if the class met only during the first hour.
  • Remote-Asynchronous Classes: Please refer to your syllabus or the instructor for details about final exams in asynchronous classes.
  • If the final examinations schedule causes students to take three or more examinations in one day, any of their instructors may arrange an examination later during finals week

6:00 - 8:00 PM		Monday
6:00 - 8:00 PM

Special Conditions:

  • Classes beginning at 6:00 p.m. or later will have their final exams during finals week at the regularly scheduled time. For example, a class that meets TR 6:00–9:00 PM will have its final exam on Tuesday of finals week from 6:00–8:00 PM. The exam period should not exceed two hours.
  • For classes involving both lecture and lab, the final exam will be based only upon the meeting times and days for the lecture portion of the class.
  • The final exam for a class that meets only as a lab should be at the scheduled time during finals week.
  • Two-hour classes and classes starting on the hour but extending beyond the next hour should schedule exams as if the class met only during the first hour.
  • Remote-Asynchronous Classes: Please refer to your syllabus or the instructor for details about final exams in asynchronous classes.
  • If the final examinations schedule causes students to take three or more examinations in one day, any of their instructors may arrange an examination later during finals week.

Make Waves.

Email: Registrar.Office@wwu.edu
Phone: (360) 650-3432
Fax: (360) 650-7327

516 High Street
Old Main 230 MS 9008

Monday–Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
